summary: A senior US official confirmed to Reuters that Israel has withdrawn from parts of the security zone in southern Lebanon, while reports indicate a principal agreement has been reached on 'pilot areas' in southern Lebanon with details expected to be finalized in Washington talks. Hezbollah warned the Lebanese government that any concession to Israel would be void, asserting that nothing passes through Lebanon without Hezbollah's understanding. Meanwhile, the commander of the IRGC Quds Force warned that Israel must leave all of Lebanon.
key_points: A senior US official confirmed to Reuters that Israel has withdrawn from parts of the security zone in southern Lebanon. (Source: @yediotnews25/131917)
A principal agreement has been reached regarding 'pilot areas' in southern Lebanon, with details expected to be finalized in Washington negotiations. (Source: @yediotnews25/131920)
An advanced draft of an understanding document between Israel and Lebanon has been reached and is expected to be published. (Source: @yediotnews25/131920)

Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities that any concession granted to Israel would be unenforceable, stating nothing passes through Lebanon without Hezbollah's agreement. (Source: @yediotnews25/131921)

IRGC Quds Force Commander Brigadier General Esmail Qaani warned that Israel must leave all of Lebanon, stating that if they do not retreat by choice, they will be forced to flee in defeat. (Source: @presstv/195569)
Israeli media reported two killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon, with Lebanon's National News Agency reporting a car was targeted near Kafr Rumman. (Source: @manniefabian/58674)
IDF Brigade 401 has been operating in southern Lebanon for three months, with its commander describing significant achievements and heavy losses. (Source: @idf_telegram/24435)
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy issued a VHF radio warning that passage through the Strait of Hormuz is only possible with IRGC Navy approval and on designated routes. (Source: @pkpoi/142967)

sections.executive_summary: A senior US official confirmed to Reuters that Israel has withdrawn from parts of the security zone in southern Lebanon, while reports indicate a principal agreement has been reached on 'pilot areas' in southern Lebanon with details expected to be finalized in Washington talks. Hezbollah warned the Lebanese government that any concession to Israel would be void, asserting that nothing passes through Lebanon without Hezbollah's understanding. Meanwhile, the commander of the IRGC Quds Force warned that Israel must leave all of Lebanon.

A principal agreement has been reached regarding 'pilot areas' in southern Lebanon, with details expected to be finalized in Washington negotiations. (Source: @yediotnews25/131920)

Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities that any concession granted to Israel would be unenforceable, stating nothing passes through Lebanon without Hezbollah's agreement. (Source: @yediotnews25/131921)

IRGC Quds Force Commander Brigadier General Esmail Qaani warned that Israel must leave all of Lebanon, stating that if they do not retreat by choice, they will be forced to flee in defeat. (Source: @presstv/195569)
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy issued a VHF radio warning that passage through the Strait of Hormuz is only possible with IRGC Navy approval and on designated routes. (Source: @pkpoi/142967)

sections.threat_assessment: The security situation in southern Lebanon remains volatile as Israel has reportedly withdrawn from parts of the security zone while negotiations continue in Washington on 'pilot areas' and an understanding document. Hezbollah has issued explicit warnings that any concessions to Israel would be unenforceable, asserting its veto power over Lebanese government decisions. The IRGC Quds Force commander's warning that Israel must leave all of Lebanon signals continued Iranian-backed resistance to any Israeli presence. The IRGC Navy's assertion of control over Strait of Hormuz passage, combined with Iran's proposal for joint service fees with Gulf neighbors, represents a significant escalation in maritime security threats.
sections.regional_response: The Gulf Cooperation Council countries have expressed serious concerns to US Secretary of State Marco Rubio regarding regional security, with Oman explicitly stating that future Strait of Hormuz arrangements do not include transit fees. The US has firmly rejected any fees for passage through the Strait of Hormuz, with Rubio stating that no country has the right to charge for use of international shipping lanes. Yemen's Ansal Allah leader has threatened to target any Israeli presence in Somaliland, calling for Red Sea coastal states to adopt a common stance against Israeli activities. Iraq is reportedly considering leaving OPEC due to economic crisis following the war in Iran and damage to its oil export capacity.
sections.intelligence_notes: The reported Israeli withdrawal from parts of the security zone in southern Lebanon, combined with the advanced draft of an understanding document, suggests significant diplomatic progress, though Hezbollah's explicit rejection of any concessions indicates implementation challenges remain. The IRGC Navy's VHF radio warning asserting control over Strait of Hormuz passage represents a concrete operational escalation beyond rhetoric, requiring monitoring of actual enforcement actions. The deletion of a Shaabi-affiliated channel's report on the death of 3 elements suggests internal information management within Iraqi militia networks. The reported killing of the Iranian hacker group 'Hantleh' commander during the recent war indicates continued Israeli covert operations against Iranian cyber capabilities.
